steps.title: Calculate base area and side length
steps.content: Total surface area equals base area plus lateral surface area. The base area is $28,160 - 16,060 = 12,100$ square inches. Since the pyramid has a square base, $s^2 = 12,100$, which gives $s = 110$ inches.

steps.title: Find the slant height from lateral surface area
steps.content:
The four lateral faces are triangles with base $s$ and height $\ell$ (the slant height). Their combined area is $2s\ell = 16,060$. With $s = 110$:
$$220\ell = 16,060$$
$$\ell = 73 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Use Pythagorean theorem to find pyramid height
steps.content:
In a right square pyramid, the slant height $\ell$, pyramid height $h$, and the distance from the base center to an edge midpoint ($s/2 = 55$) form a right triangle. By the Pythagorean theorem:
$$h^2 + 55^2 = 73^2$$
$$h^2 + 3,025 = 5,329$$
$$h^2 = 2,304$$
$$h = 48 \text{ inches}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
